exclusion_rules.description: Free services only at government health facilities (PHC, CHC, sub-district, district hospitals). Private hospitals are NOT covered.
Sick newborn entitlements apply only up to 1 year (365 days) from birth

family_criteria.family_definition: Covers the pregnant woman (any age) and her newborn child up to 1 year of age. No family size, income, or caste restriction. Universal entitlement at government facilities.

covered_procedures: Complicated deliveries and management — completely free
All drugs and consumables during delivery and hospital stay
All diagnostic tests (blood tests, ultrasound, X-ray, etc.)
Blood transfusion if required (free of cost)
Free transport from home to facility (referral transport)
Free transport between facilities in case of referral
Free drop-back from facility to home after discharge
Exemption from all user charges at government facilities
Sick newborn treatment up to 30 days at facility
Sick newborn: free drugs, consumables, diagnostics, blood
Sick newborn: free transport and diet for mother accompanying child
excluded_procedures: Delivery at private hospitals or non-government facilities
Treatment of newborns older than 1 year
Maternal health issues unrelated to pregnancy/delivery
Post-natal complications beyond the initial hospital stay period

required_documents.name: MCH Card / ANC Registration
required_documents.notes: Preferred but not mandatory — no pregnant woman can be denied free delivery at a government facility

required_documents.name: Aadhaar Card
required_documents.notes: For record-keeping; absence of ID cannot be reason for denial of services

required_documents.name: Referral Slip
required_documents.notes: For inter-facility referral transport. Emergency cases accepted without referral.

enrollment_steps: Step 1: No prior enrollment needed — entitlement is automatic at any government health facility
Step 2: Visit nearest government hospital when delivery is due (or call 108/102 for free ambulance)
Step 3: Present any available ID at admission (not mandatory — cannot be denied without it)
Step 4: Receive completely free delivery care including drugs, diagnostics, diet, and blood
Step 5: If referral needed, free transport arranged to higher facility
Step 6: After discharge, free drop-back transport to home arranged
Step 7: For sick newborn, return to government facility within 1 year for free treatment
description_for_embedding: Janani Shishu Suraksha Karyakram (JSSK) is a central government entitlement scheme that guarantees completely free delivery services (normal and C-section) to ALL pregnant women at government health facilities, regardless of income, caste, or BPL status. It covers free drugs, diagnostics, diet, blood transfusion, and transport (home to facility, between facilities, and drop-back). Sick newborns up to 1 year also get free treatment. Unlike JSY which gives cash, JSSK eliminates out-of-pocket expenses by making all services free. No enrollment or documents required.
